title: Event Hub: Providing Partition Key in Event Metadata Not Working
body:
## In what area(s)?

/area runtime

## What version of Dapr?

1.10.5

## Expected Behavior

Using the Dapr .NET SDK, I am trying to bulk publish events to an Azure event hub, while providing a partition key. I am expecting my event hub to receive events whose metadata includes this partition key.

## Actual Behavior

Events received by the event hub do not have a partition key in their metadata.


## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

```
var metadata = new Dictionary<string, string>
{
    ["partitionKey"] = <partition key>
};

await _daprClient.BulkPublishEventAsync(<pub sub name>, <event hub name>, telemetryMessages, metadata);
```

## Release Note

RELEASE NOTE:

comments.body:
@yaron2 from our prior investigation into this in Discord this is an implementation issue in either DotNet SDK or runtime.

@mukundansundar please take a look again and transfer to the appropriate repo. Please do also double-check the EventHubs implementation though that does not seem to be the issue.

This works without the Bulk Operations and the Bulk Operations are Alpha APIs right now. Therefore this will not be a release blocker.

@mukundansundar please do investigate what is going on here.

cc @DeepanshuA (and ping @mukundansundar )

This issue is about DotNet SDK + EventHubs PubSub. Metadata (here the crucial eventhubs partition key) does not appear to be applied correctly during bulk publish.
